infobox.value: ([Croatian](/wiki/Croatian_language "Croatian language"): *Sveučilište u Zagrebu*, [Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Universitas Studiorum Zagrabiensis*) is a [public](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") [research university](/wiki/Research_university "Research university") in [Zagreb](/wiki/Zagreb "Zagreb"), Croatia. It is the largest Croatian university and [one of the oldest continuously operating universities in Europe](/wiki/List_of_oldest_universities_in_continuous_operation "List of oldest universities in continuous operation").[[5]](#cite_note-5) The University of Zagreb and the [University North](/wiki/University_North "University North") are the only public universities operating in [Northern](/wiki/Northern_Croatia "Northern Croatia") and [Central Croatia](/wiki/Central_Croatia "Central Croatia").

The **University of Zagreb** ([Croatian](/wiki/Croatian_language "Croatian language"): *Sveučilište u Zagrebu*, [Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Universitas Studiorum Zagrabiensis*) is a [public](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") [research university](/wiki/Research_university "Research university") in [Zagreb](/wiki/Zagreb "Zagreb"), Croatia. It is the largest Croatian university and [one of the oldest continuously operating universities in Europe](/wiki/List_of_oldest_universities_in_continuous_operation "List of oldest universities in continuous operation").[[5]](#cite_note-5) The University of Zagreb and the [University North](/wiki/University_North "University North") are the only public universities operating in [Northern](/wiki/Northern_Croatia "Northern Croatia") and [Central Croatia](/wiki/Central_Croatia "Central Croatia").

The history of the University began on 23 September 1669, when the [Holy Roman Emperor](/wiki/Holy_Roman_Emperor "Holy Roman Emperor") [Leopold I](/wiki/Leopold_I,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Leopold I, Holy Roman Emperor") issued a decree granting the establishment of the *Jesuit Academy of the Royal Free City of Zagreb*. The decree was accepted at the Council of the [Croatian Kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia_(Habsburg) "Kingdom of Croatia (Habsburg)") on 3 November 1671. The Academy was run by the [Jesuits](/wiki/Jesuits "Jesuits") for more than a century until the order was dissolved by [Pope Clement XIV](/wiki/Pope_Clement_XIV "Pope Clement XIV") in 1773. In 1776, Empress [Maria Theresa](/wiki/Maria_Theresa "Maria Theresa") issued a decree founding the *Royal Academy of Science* which succeeded the previous Jesuit Academy. Bishop [Josip Juraj Strossmayer](/wiki/Josip_Juraj_Strossmayer "Josip Juraj Strossmayer") proposed the founding of a University to the [Croatian Parliament](/wiki/Croatian_Parliament "Croatian Parliament") in 1861. Emperor [Franz Joseph](/wiki/Franz_Joseph_I_of_Austria "Franz Joseph I of Austria") signed the decree on the establishment of the University of Zagreb in 1869. The Act of Founding was passed by the Parliament in 1874, and was ratified by the Emperor on 5 January 1874. On 19 October 1874 the *Royal University of Franz Joseph I* was officially opened.

The University is composed of 29 faculties, 3 art academies and 1 university center with more than 70,000 students.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Zagreb&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Academy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Zagreb&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Academy")]

The beginnings of the later university date back to 23 September 1669 when Emperor and King [Leopold I](/wiki/Leopold_I,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Leopold I, Holy Roman Emperor") Habsburg issued a decree granting the establishment of the [Jesuit](/wiki/Jesuit "Jesuit") Academy of the Royal Free City of [Zagreb](/wiki/Zagreb "Zagreb").[[6]](#cite_note-Rüegg_685-6) According to that document the study of philosophy in Zagreb acquired a formal and legal status as *Neoacademia Zagrabiensis* and officially became a public institution of higher education.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8b/Zillingtal_-_Pfarrkirche_hl._Petrus_und_Paulus_%2804%29.jpg/250px-Zillingtal_-_Pfarrkirche_hl._Petrus_und_Paulus_%2804%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Zillingtal_-_Pfarrkirche_hl._Petrus_und_Paulus_(04).jpg)

Filip Kaušić (1618–1673) was the first rector of the Academy

The academy was run by the Jesuits for more than a century until the order was dissolved by Pope [Clement XIV](/wiki/Clement_XIV "Clement XIV") in 1773. Under a new leadership in 1772 the academy enrolled a total of 200 students.

In 1776 Empress and Queen [Maria Theresa](/wiki/Maria_Theresa_of_Austria "Maria Theresa of Austria") issued a decree founding the Royal Academy of Science ([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Regia Scientiarum Academia*).[[6]](#cite_note-Rüegg_685-6) It consisted of three studies or [faculties](/wiki/Faculty_(division) "Faculty (division)") of philosophy, theology, and law. The former political-[cameral](/wiki/Cameral "Cameral") studies became part of the newly established faculty of law, and thus were integrated into the academy. Each of the faculties of the Royal Academy of Sciences had several chairs teaching one or several courses. During the [Austro-Turkish War of 1788–1791](/wiki/Austro-Turkish_War_(1788%E2%80%931791) "Austro-Turkish War (1788–1791)") and following the Austrian occupation of [Belgrade](/wiki/Belgrade "Belgrade") on 8 October 1789 the Royal Academy requested to be granted the university status with the following argumentation:

> *If we consider the current circumstances in which serious consideration is given not only to the removal of obstacles to science, but also to the appointment of our local citizens to all services in [these kingdoms](/wiki/Triune_Kingdom_of_Croatia,_Slavonia_and_Dalmatia "Triune Kingdom of Croatia, Slavonia and Dalmatia"), and if we also consider for a moment future opportunities, not only that the part of Croatia that is still sobbing under the Turkish yoke but also the kingdoms of Bosnia and [Serbia](/wiki/Habsburg-occupied_Serbia_(1788%E2%80%931792) "Habsburg-occupied Serbia (1788–1792)") – [...] – will be liberated and that these glorious kingdoms will be almost expanded. We believe that it is not only proud and useful, but also absolutely necessary to have in our environment such a university in which – when all obstacles to scientific work are removed and when funds for its development are obtained – number of local youth will be educated in all the sciences and noble skills for performing various services in their homeland.*[[7]](#cite_note-Jugoslavija_u_istorijskoj_perspektivi-7)

The academy in Zagreb remained until 1874, despite numerous organizational changes, the focal institution of higher education in Croatia, educating most of the members of the Croatian intelligentsia.

### University

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Zagreb&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: University")]

See also: [Franz Joseph University (disambiguation)](/wiki/Franz_Joseph_University_(disambiguation) "Franz Joseph University (disambiguation)")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Matija_Mesi%C4%87.jpg/250px-Matija_Mesi%C4%87.jpg)](/wiki/File:Matija_Mesi%C4%87.jpg)

[Matija Mesić](/wiki/Matija_Mesi%C4%87 "Matija Mesić"), first rector of the University

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3a/Promotion_of_new_PhDs.jpg/250px-Promotion_of_new_PhDs.jpg)](/wiki/File:Promotion_of_new_PhDs.jpg)

Promotion of new PhDs in 2015

Bishop [Josip Juraj Strossmayer](/wiki/Josip_Juraj_Strossmayer "Josip Juraj Strossmayer") in 1861 proposed to the [Croatian Parliament](/wiki/Croatian_Parliament "Croatian Parliament") the founding of a university at Zagreb. During his visit in 1869, the Emperor [Franz Joseph](/wiki/Franz_Joseph "Franz Joseph") signed the decree on the establishment of the University of Zagreb. Five years later, the Parliament passed the Act of Founding, which was ratified by the Emperor on 5 January 1874. On 19 October 1874, a ceremony was held in the name of the founding of the *Royal University of Franz Joseph I in Zagreb*,[[8]](#cite_note-8) making it the third university in the [Hungarian realm](/wiki/Transleithania "Transleithania") of the [Austro-Hungarian Empire](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Empire "Austro-Hungarian Empire").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 1874 the University had four faculties:

* Law (*Pravno-državoslovni fakultet*)
* Theology (*Bogoslovni fakultet*)
* Philosophy (*Mudroslovni fakultet*)
* Medicine (*Liječnički fakultet*)

The Faculty of Medicine was not put into function in 1874; it had to wait until 1917. The Faculty of Philosophy served as the general scientific faculty. Since 1876 it had geology, botany, physics, mathematics, and chemistry; since 1877 zoology; since 1882 pharmacy; since 1883 geography.

In 1860, the [Royal Agriculture and Forestry College](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Agriculture_and_Forestry_College&action=edit&redlink=1 "Royal Agriculture and Forestry College (page does not exist)") was founded in [Križevci](/wiki/Kri%C5%BEevci,_Croatia "Križevci, Croatia").[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 1898, the Academy of Forestry (*Šumarska akademija*) was founded as part of the Faculty of Philosophy, which encompassed all technical studies. In 1919, this school became the Faculty of Husbandry and Forestry.

In 1919, the School of Technology (*Tehnička visoka škola*) was founded, which was transformed into a university faculty in 1926. Also in 1919 the School of Veterinary Medicine (*Veterinarska visoka škola*) was founded; it transformed into a university faculty in 1925. From 1920 to 1924 shortly existed Faculty of Eastern Orthodox Theology.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/01/Great_Hall_of_the_Rectorate_of_the_University_of_Zagreb.jpg/250px-Great_Hall_of_the_Rectorate_of_the_University_of_Zagreb.jpg)](/wiki/File:Great_Hall_of_the_Rectorate_of_the_University_of_Zagreb.jpg)

Great Hall of the University

In the Faculty of Philosophy, major reorganization ensued in the 1920s, as mathematics, pharmacy and other sciences started to split off, first with the creation of separate mathematics and pharmaceutical departments in 1928, when the faculty was renamed into its current name *Filozofski fakultet*.

In 1926, the university was composed of seven faculties:

* Theology (*Bogoslovni fakultet*)
* Law (*Pravnički fakultet*)
* Medicine (*Liječnički fakultet*)
* Philosophy (*Mudroslovni fakultet*)
  + Philosophy dept. (*Filozofski odjel*)
  + Pharmacy dept. (*Farmaceutski odjel*)
* Husbandry and Forestry (*Gospodarsko-šumarski fakultet*)
* Veterinary Medicine (*Veterinarski fakultet*)
* Technology (*Tehnički fakultet*)
  + Construction dept. (*Građevni odsjek*)
  + Engineering dept. (*Strojarski odsjek*)
  + Chemical engineering dept. (*Kemijsko-inženjerski odsjek*)

On 26 August 1936 a group of Macedonian students belonging to the [MANAPO](/wiki/MANAPO "MANAPO") signed the *Political Declaration*, an illegal document requesting political and social emancipation of [Macedonians](/wiki/Macedonians_(ethnic_group) "Macedonians (ethnic group)") in the [Kingdom of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Yugoslavia "Kingdom of Yugoslavia").[[12]](#cite_note-Jugoslavija_u_istorijskoj_perspektivi-Jančeva&Litovski-12)

During the [Independent State of Croatia](/wiki/Independent_State_of_Croatia "Independent State of Croatia") (1941–1945), the university was known as the *Croatian University* (Hrvatsko sveučilište).

The individual departments of the Faculty of Philosophy became separate faculties in 1942, 1946 when the Faculty of Sciences was formed, and finally in 1963.

In 1956, the Faculty of Technology was divided into four faculties:

* Architecture-Construction-Geodesy (*Arhitektonsko-građevinsko-geodetski fakultet*)
* Electrical engineering (*Elektrotehnički fakultet*)
* Mechanical engineering-Shipbuilding (*Strojarsko-brodograđevni fakultet*)
* Chemistry-Food technology-Mining (*Tehnološki fakultet*)

These eventually split up into the current layout.

In 1999, the University decided to implement [European Credit Transfer System](/wiki/European_Credit_Transfer_System "European Credit Transfer System") – ECTS. When Croatia signed to be a part of The [Bologna declaration](/wiki/Bologna_declaration "Bologna declaration"), all of the [universities in Croatia](/wiki/Universities_in_Croatia "Universities in Croatia") adopted this system of easily readable and comparable degrees.

University offers 160 undergraduate programmes (ba/bsc), 22 integrated undergraduate-graduate programmes, 9 vocational undergraduate programmes, 174 graduate programmes (ma/msc), 1 vocational graduate programme, 72 doctoral programmes (PhD) and 165 specialist postgraduate programmes.

**Faculty of Philosophy and Religious Sciences (FFRZ)** is a part of the University of Zagreb, [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia") It remains a work of the [Society of Jesus](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") and traces its origins to 1662.

FFRZ in Zagreb began as a Jesuit school of philosophy on 6 November 1662 with the establishment of the Philosophy Department at Zagreb College, which would become the University of Zagreb.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

The **Faculty of Philosophy of the Society of Jesus** (FFDI) closed in 1773 due to the suppression of the [Society of Jesus](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus"), and the Jesuit philosophy school in Zagreb did not reopen until 1937, when it offered a three-year course leading to the licentiate in philosophy, as it does today.[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14)

On 31 July 1989 the Congregation for Catholic Education of the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") decreed that the Faculty of Philosophy could confer the [baccalaureate](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degree "Bachelor's degree"), [licentiate](/wiki/Licentiate_(degree) "Licentiate (degree)"), and doctorate in philosophy.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

With a decision of 7 October 1992, the Ministry of Science and Technology entered FFDI into the register as a Scientific Research Organization in philosophy and it became a part of the [Croatian Studies](/wiki/Croatian_Studies "Croatian Studies") Department of the University of Zagreb.

On 8 December 2016, the Senate of the University of Zagreb determined that FFDI would become a faculty and equal component of the University under the title Faculty of Philosophy and Religious Sciences (FFRZ).[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[14]](#cite_note-:0-14)

Undergraduates may major in Philosophy and Religious Sciences, Philosophy, or Philosophy and Latin Language. Graduate students may major in Philosophy or Religious Science. FFRZ also offers post-graduate studies.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

FFRZ has a formal relationship with [Laudato TV](/wiki/Laudato_TV "Laudato TV") to "work together to promote and implement educational, cultural and scientific activities in the Christian and humanistic atmosphere".[[18]](#cite_note-18)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6d/University_of_Zagreb.jpg/250px-University_of_Zagreb.jpg)](/wiki/File:University_of_Zagreb.jpg)

Palace of the University and the [Faculty of Law](/wiki/Faculty_of_Law,_University_of_Zagreb "Faculty of Law, University of Zagreb"), [Republic of Croatia Square](/wiki/Republic_of_Croatia_Square "Republic of Croatia Square")

In 2017 there were two current research studies at the Faculty of Philosophy and Religious Sciences:

* [Practical ethics](/wiki/Practical_ethics "Practical ethics") and commitment to the common good in times of crisis. Is there a place for virtue in Croatian society?
* [Christian philosophy](/wiki/Christian_philosophy "Christian philosophy") within the Croatian philosophy of the 20th century.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

On 5 May 2017 a symposium was held on "Religions and Migration: Displaced Persons and Refugees".[[20]](#cite_note-20)

The faculty is led by a chancellor and his deputy along with a dean and vice-dean. The chancellor is [Arturo Sosa](/wiki/Arturo_Sosa "Arturo Sosa"), General Superior of the Society of Jesus based in Rome. His deputy is Dalibor Renić, Provincial Superior of the Croatian Province of the Society of Jesus based in Zagreb. The dean is Prof. Ivan Koprek,[[21]](#cite_note-21) The Faculty Council is composed of all regular and extraordinary professors and the Faculty Conference includes all current lecturers, student representatives, and faculty officials.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Zagreb&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Legacy")]

Since 1874, more than 200,000 students have received a bachelor's degree, more than 18,000 a master's, and more than 8,000 a doctorate from the University of Zagreb.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Zagreb&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Sports")]

The University of Zagreb was a co-organiser (with the [University of Rijeka](/wiki/University_of_Rijeka "University of Rijeka")) of the [2016 European Universities Games](/wiki/2016_European_Universities_Games "2016 European Universities Games").

The university was also awarded by [EUSA](/wiki/European_University_Sports_Association "European University Sports Association") as the best (2016, 2019, 2023) and the most active European university in sport activities (2019 and 2023).[[34]](#cite_note-34)

### Rector's award

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Zagreb&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Rector's award")]

The Rector's award (*Rektorova nagrada*) is awarded as part of the University of Zagreb Week (*Tjedan Sveučilišta u Zagrebu*) in November. The award committee consists of two professors from each of the seven [scientific fields](/wiki/Scientific_field "Scientific field") covered by the University.[[35]](#cite_note-35)
